what_it_does: Declares a planned UI surface specification (B7) for the 'decide-for-me autonomous routing' view, binding it to journey J4, three ui:// addresses, and the board/chat/toolbar components, while prescribing build outputs and design-token sources.
observations: Surface id is B7 in area B with status planned and home journey J4.
Represents two labels: RHM-decide and GOV-tiers.
Maps to three ui:// addresses: board/decide, chat/approve, toolbar/presence/decide-for-me.
build_to names components board, chat, and toolbar; a design_lint command; and a tokens source path.
journeys contains only J4 and cross_journeys is empty.
The _what field states it was generated by a Claude Code session and warns never to invent an address.
standouts: A single view is exposed through three distinct UI addresses across board, chat, and toolbar.
Build instructions include a lint gate and a single token source.
It explicitly represents two domain concepts (RHM-decide, GOV-tiers).
conventions: Follows a structured surface-spec schema with id, area, title, status, platforms, journeys, and addresses.
Uses a ui:// address scheme to locate surfaces in the UI.
References journeys and cross_journeys as address-carrying web refs.
Includes self-documenting provenance in a _what field.
Build targets are separated into components, lint, and tokens.
concerns: Status is 'planned' with no owner, timeline, or acceptance criteria.
cross_journeys is empty despite the _what text describing it as part of the web.
The design_lint command is a literal shell string that may drift if paths change.
The tokens path contains an arrow character, making it more human-readable than machine-parseable.
notes: This is a planned surface spec that pins the 'decide-for-me' view to three UI addresses and a concrete build pipeline.
questions: How do the three ui:// addresses coordinate within one view?
What gates the move from 'planned' to built?
Is the design_lint command intended for manual use or CI integration?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to specify a generated surface for view B7; the file does that, listing addresses, journeys, and build targets consistently.
apparent_themes: Build traceability from spec to components and tokens.
Separation of representational concerns via RHM/GOV labels.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make a view's UI footprint explicit and machine-actionable by linking a conceptual feature ('decide-for-me autonomous routing') to exact component addresses, a lint gate, and a single token source.
novelty: The 'decide-for-me autonomous routing' title and three-address surface footprint across board, chat, and toolbar suggest a cross-surface autonomous-routing feature rather than a conventional single-page view.
contribution: This file contributes a concrete, address-bound surface specification that a fused system could keep as a record of where the 'decide-for-me' feature surfaces, which components it builds to, and which design tokens govern its look.
summary_for_embedding: B7.json is a planned surface specification for the 'decide-for-me autonomous routing' view in area B, journey J4, mapping to three ui:// addresses across board, chat, and toolbar and prescribing a build pipeline via components, lint, and tokens.
intention_for_embedding: The file was intended to record the exact UI addresses, journey affiliation, and build targets for a planned autonomous-routing surface so that code and design substrates stay aligned.
